revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Furutsuki Yokoanabos]] {{Short description|Archaeological site in Fukuoka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Furutsuki Yokoanabo Cluster | native_name = 古月横穴 | native_name_lang = | image =Furutsuki Yokoana.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Furutsuki Yokoanabo Cluster | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Furutsuki Yokoanabo Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|48|14|N|130|40|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|Kurate, Fukuoka|qid=Q629799|lt=Kurate, Fukuoka|1=Kurate, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|48|14|N|130|40|09|E}}}}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Furutsuki Yokoanabo Cluster'''|古月横穴|Furutsuki yokoana}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|qid=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=Yokoanabo|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in the town of {{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|Kurate, Fukuoka|qid=Q629799|lt=Kurate, Fukuoka|1=Kurate, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Kurate District, Fukuoka|en|Kurate District, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206433|lt=Kurate District|1=Kurate District, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was granted protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1932, with the area under protection expanded in 1986.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139697|title=古月横穴 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=5 December 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Furutsuki Yokoanabo Cluster is a group of horizontal cave tombs located on the slope of a hill on the west bank of the Nishi River, which flows into the Onga River. Discovered in 1926, tombs were designated as a National Historic Site in 1932, and subsequent [[archaeological excavation]]s confirmed a total 41 tombs on three slopes, east, west, and south slopes of the hill, including the original 13. The cave-shaped tombs are carved into the hard rock of a hill, and are thought to have been built over a period of about 100 years from the late 6th century to the late 7th century as the tombs for an important local clan. The Furutsuki site is unusual among horizontal tombs, in that it has decorative patterns on the walls inside the tombs; Tomb No. 9 has a pattern of diagonal and horizontal lines is drawn in red, and No. 9 has a diagonal lattice pattern painted in vermilion. It can be assumed that there were trends in construction methods depending on the era, such as the entrance to the side cave having an eave stone, and the size of the tomb chambers varying in size. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} such as {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, iron swords, {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|qid=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness|1=Horse harness}}es, and earrings were unearthed from almost all of the graves. The site has now been maintained as a public park, and relics are displayed and preserved at the Kurate Town History and Folklore Museum. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is approximately 4.3 kilometers west of {{ill|Kurate Station|en|Kurate Station|qid=Q11663882|lt=Kurate Station|1=Kurate Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhō Main Line|en|Chikuhō Main Line|qid=Q872540|lt=Chikuhō Main Line|1=Chikuhō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
